rationale: High social service nonprofit density increases fundraising competition, lowering efficiency.
prior_art_summary: Existing research on nonprofit competition suggests that clustering intensifies donor solicitation efforts, raising fundraising expenses relative to contributions.

rationale: High local real estate prices increase overhead costs, reducing fundraising efficiency.
prior_art_summary: Spatial mismatch literature traditionally focuses on employment and housing, not nonprofit cost-efficiency. Highly novel application.

rationale: High poverty rate ZIPs have lower local donor capital, raising fundraising costs.
prior_art_summary: Socioeconomic conditions of local markets are well-established determinants of donor responses and fundraising efficiency.

rationale: Winsorized efficiency shows robust negative correlation with local branch density.
prior_art_summary: Winsorization is a standard econometric approach to deal with outliers, confirming spatial competition effects.

rationale: Winsorized efficiency is lower in high-cost ZIP codes (testing spatial mismatch).
prior_art_summary: Confirms the spatial mismatch hypothesis using robust outlier-capped DVs.
